# Architecture 2.0 Flowchart {#sec-appendix-d-flowchart}

```{=latex}
\abstract*{This appendix provides a visual flowchart and cheat sheet summarizing the entire Architecture 2.0 loop—from the initial study brief to the final human architect sign-off.}
```

The Architecture 2.0 loop relies on the strict separation of delegated work (performed by models, tools, and scripts) and assigned responsibility (owned by the human architect). The following flowchart illustrates how the 8 distinct roles (Generation, Prediction, Optimization, Critique, Repair, Verification, Explanation, Coordination) interact throughout the design process, culminating in the architect's final sign-off.

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-arch-loop-flowchart
%%| fig-cap: "The Architecture 2.0 Loop. Models and tools execute candidates, but the human architect owns the intent, evidence boundary, and final decision."

graph TD
    %% Define Styles
    classDef human fill:#d4e6f1,stroke:#2980b9,stroke-width:2px,color:#000
    classDef aiRole fill:#e8daef,stroke:#8e44ad,stroke-width:2px,color:#000
    classDef tool fill:#d5f5e3,stroke:#27ae60,stroke-width:2px,color:#000
    classDef review fill:#fcf3cf,stroke:#f1c40f,stroke-width:2px,color:#000

    %% Nodes
    A["Human Architect<br>(Defines Intent & Scope)"]:::human
    B["Coordination<br>(Routes Tasks)"]:::aiRole
    C["Generation<br>(Proposes Candidates)"]:::aiRole
    D["Prediction & Simulation<br>(Evaluates Hardware Proxies)"]:::tool
    E{"Meets Objectives?"}:::review
    F["Critique & Repair<br>(Diagnoses Failures)"]:::aiRole
    G["Optimization<br>(Refines Parameters)"]:::aiRole
    H["Verification & Explanation<br>(Checks Bounds & Explains)"]:::tool
    I["Evidence Review<br>(Card & Signoff)"]:::review
    J["Accountable Decision<br>(Human Final Sign-Off)"]:::human

    %% Flow
    A -->|1. Prompts & Study Brief| B
    B -->|2. Delegates Work| C
    C -->|3. Submits Candidate| D
    D -->|4. Proxy Result| E
    E -- "No" --> F
    F -->|5. Revised Candidate| D
    E -- "Yes" --> G
    G -->|6. Tuned Candidate| H
    H -->|7. Formal Checks & Logs| I
    I -->|8. Presents Evidence| J
    A -.->|Retains Final Authority| J
```

## The 8 AI Roles in Practice

1. **Coordination:** Interprets the architect's study brief and allocates tasks.
2. **Generation:** Creates candidates, configurations, and Verilog blocks.
3. **Prediction:** Estimates performance and power using surrogate models.
4. **Optimization:** Performs parameter sweeps and design-space exploration.
5. **Critique:** Identifies violations in design rules or constraints.
6. **Repair:** Generates fixes based on critique or simulation failures.
7. **Verification:** Validates memory coherence, functional correctness, and physical rules.
8. **Explanation:** Summarizes simulation telemetry and causality for human review.

This explicit structure ensures that we never treat the generation of a candidate as the validation of an architecture. The truth is established in the simulation and verification flow, and the final decision is assigned strictly to the human architect.
